signedReleases.note: npm package published; check npm provenance attestations on the latest version.

codeReview.note: Active Cloudflare team maintains; PRs go through Cloudflare's internal review.

secretsHandling.note: API token via env. Server doesn't persist or log it.

oauthHandling.note: Cloudflare's API tokens are best-in-class fine-grained: per-account, per-zone, per-permission-group. Use them.

filesystemAccess.note: Some Workers deploy tools may read script files from a configured directory; scope at the host.

telemetry.note: Cloudflare's server-side audit log captures API calls (governed by Cloudflare's privacy policy).

dataResidency.note: Cloudflare's infrastructure. Specific resources (R2, Workers KV, D1) have their own residency settings.

defaultPermissions.note: Per the API token. Default templates over-grant; trim before issuing.

destructiveTools.note: Cache purges, DNS edits, and Workers deploys are immediately effectful. No dry-run for most; gate at the host.

promptInjectionExposure.note: Tool output (Workers script source, KV values, R2 object metadata) can contain hostile content. Lower exposure than browser/filesystem MCPs because tool surface is more API-shaped.

rateLimitRisk.note: Cloudflare API rate limits are generous for normal use; only burst-deploy patterns hit ceilings.

verdictScope: Cloudflare infrastructure operations using a fine-grained API token scoped per-purpose.
verdictNote: Use it. Token scoping is the strongest safety lever — start from the minimum permission-group template and trim. Rotate tokens monthly. Watch Cloudflare's audit log on the same cadence you check IAM logs elsewhere.
